problem_statement: The force P is applied to the 50-kg block at rest on a 15 degree incline. Determine the friction force if P = 200 N applied at 20 degrees.
solution_steps: Sum forces perpendicular: N - 491cos15 + 200sin20 = 0, N = 405N
Sum forces parallel: 200cos20 - 491sin15 - F = 0, F = 61.0N
Check F_max = mu_s * N = 101.3N > 61.0N so equilibrium holds

problem_statement: A crate rests on a horizontal surface with coefficient of static friction 0.3. Find the minimum horizontal force to start moving a 100 kg crate.
Find the centroid of a composite area made of a rectangle and a semicircle.
solution_steps: Split into rectangle and semicircle, compute individual centroids and areas
Use weighted average formula xbar = sum(x_i*A_i)/sum(A_i)

problem_statement: The 30-kg homogeneous cylinder of 400-mm diameter rests against vertical and inclined surfaces. The coefficient of static friction between the cylinder and the surfaces is 0.30. Calculate the applied clockwise couple M which would cause the cylinder to slip.
solution_steps: Sum moments about G: M - 0.3(NA+NB)(0.2) = 0
Sum Fx=0: NB sin30 + 0.3 NB cos30 - NA = 0
Solving Eqs. (1)-(3): NB = 312 N, NA = 237 N, M = 32.9 N.m

topic: friction - wheel rolling on circular incline
problem_statement: The 50-kg wheel rolls on its hub up the circular incline under the action of the 12-kg cylinder attached to a cord around the rim. Determine the angle theta at which the wheel comes to rest, assuming friction is sufficient to prevent slippage. What is the minimum coefficient of static friction which will permit this position to be reached with no slipping?
solution_steps: Sum moments about contact point C: 50(9.81)(75 sin theta) - 12(9.81)(200 - 75 sin theta) = 0
Solving gives theta = 31.1 degrees
Minimum friction coefficient: mu_min = tan(theta) = tan(31.1) = 0.603

topic: friction - bar with roller equilibrium angle
problem_statement: The uniform slender bar has an ideal roller at its upper end A. Determine the minimum value of the angle theta for which equilibrium is possible for mu_s = 0.25 and for mu_s = 0.50.
solution_steps: Sum Fx=0: NA cos15 - mu_s NB = 0
Sum Fy=0: NB + NA sin15 - mg = 0
Sum moments about A: -mg(L/2)cos(theta) + NB L cos(theta) - mu_s NB L sin(theta) = 0
Eliminating NA and NB gives tan(theta) = (1 - mu_s tan15) / (2 mu_s)
final_answer: For mu_s = 0.25, theta = 61.8 degrees. For mu_s = 0.50, theta = 40.9 degrees

topic: friction - block on incline with hanging mass via pulley
problem_statement: Determine the range of mass m2 for which the system is in equilibrium. The coefficient of static friction between the block and the incline is mu_s = 0.25. Neglect friction associated with the pulley.
solution_steps: Case (a), motion impends up the incline: sum Fx=0: -mu_s N - m1 g sin20 - m2 g sin20 + m2 g = 0
Sum Fy=0: N - m1 g cos20 - m2 g cos20 = 0
Solving gives m2 = 1.364 m1
Case (b), motion impends down the incline: mu_s N - (m1+m2) g sin20 + m2 g = 0, Fy equation unchanged
Solving gives m2 = 0.1199 m1

topic: friction - person pulling a loaded cart up incline
problem_statement: An 82-kg man pulls a 45-kg cart up the incline at steady speed. Determine the minimum coefficient mu_s of static friction for which the man's shoes will not slip. Also determine the distance s required for equilibrium of the man's body.
solution_steps: Incline angle alpha = tan^-1(3/10) = 16.70 degrees
Cart equilibrium: -Tcos40 + 45(9.81)sin(alpha) = 0 gives T = 165.6 N
Man's equilibrium, sum Fx: -F + 82(9.81)sin(alpha) + 165.6cos40 = 0 gives F = 358 N
Sum Fy: N - 82(9.81)cos(alpha) - 165.6sin40 = 0 gives N = 877 N
Taking moments about A gives s = 126.2 mm

topic: friction - stacked blocks with multiple friction coefficients
problem_statement: Determine the horizontal force P required to cause slippage to occur. The friction coefficients for the three pairs of mating surfaces are indicated. The top block is free to move vertically.
solution_steps: Case (a), middle block moves, bottom does not: P = 0.6(981) + 0.4(981+490.5) = 1177 N
Case (b), bottom block moves with middle block: P = 0.6(981) + 0.3(981+490.5+196.2) = 1089 N
Since 1089 < 1177, case (b) governs
